market_demands.name: Cross-system data integration
market_demands.description: Improving data integration between systems, eliminating silos between BIM, scheduling, cost, and document management tools
market_demands.survey_evidence: 56% named 'Improving data integration between systems' as #1 digital priority. Dominant friction point: 'between software systems'.
market_demands.vitus_capability: Native ACC integration with auto-sync. REST API for custom data. But limited to ACC ecosystem only.
market_demands.gap: No multi-CDE support. Users on Dalux, Projectwise, Trimble Connect, Thinkproject are unreachable. Market wants one layer across all systems.

market_demands.name: BIM data communication for non-BIM users
market_demands.description: Making BIM data accessible and understandable to project managers, clients, and field workers who don't use BIM tools
market_demands.survey_evidence: Average effectiveness score 3.5/10 for communicating with non-BIM users. Every respondent flagged this. Quotes: 'BIM tools are designed for those who already know BIM', 'licenses not available for non-BIM users', 'requires too much manual translation'.
market_demands.vitus_capability: Web-based viewer (no install needed), interactive dashboards, colorize by property, saved views with comments. One respondent specifically mentioned Vitus as their tool.
market_demands.gap: Could be stronger with simplified report generation, role-based views that auto-filter complexity, and export to non-BIM formats.

market_demands.name: Data standards and governance
market_demands.description: Building and enforcing BIM data standards, EIR compliance, and information requirement validation
market_demands.survey_evidence: 28% named 'Building data standards and governance' as top priority. Multiple respondents want automated EIR/AIR validation. ISO 19650 process quality is a constant struggle.
market_demands.vitus_capability: Document log generation for ISO 19650 compliance. Display filter for checking property completeness.
market_demands.gap: No automated EIR validation, no data quality scoring against requirements, no compliance dashboards showing gap-to-standard.

market_demands.name: Model-based cost controls (5D)
market_demands.description: Linking BIM model data to cost estimation, quantity takeoff, and budget tracking
market_demands.survey_evidence: 48% expect model-based cost controls to be standard by 2027. Multiple requests for automated quantity takeoff and cross-project cost estimation.
market_demands.vitus_capability: Quantity takeoff via BIM properties. Calculated columns in dashboards. Custom data for progress tracking.
market_demands.gap: No native cost linking, no 5D workflow, no budget-to-model comparison. Quantity takeoff exists but isn't positioned as cost control.

market_demands.name: Near real-time data sync between office and field
market_demands.description: Live synchronization of BIM data, progress, and issues between office platforms and field/site teams
market_demands.survey_evidence: 52% expect real-time sync to be standard by 2027. 'Between field and office' is a top friction point. Multiple respondents want field workers accessing model data.
market_demands.vitus_capability: Web-based viewer accessible on any device. Real-time notifications on saved views.
market_demands.gap: No dedicated field app, no offline mode, no reality capture integration, no site-to-model feedback loop.

market_demands.name: Automated progress tracking from reality capture
market_demands.description: Using laser scans, photogrammetry, or drone data to automatically compare as-built vs. as-designed
market_demands.survey_evidence: 40% expect this to be standard by 2027. Multiple respondents want planned vs. reality tracking.
market_demands.gap: Full gap. Would require point cloud ingestion, scan-to-BIM comparison, and deviation reporting.

market_demands.name: Federated CDE across partners
market_demands.description: A unified data layer that works across multiple CDEs used by different project partners
market_demands.survey_evidence: 44% expect federated CDE to be standard by 2027. Multi-CDE usage is the norm: most respondents use ACC + Dalux or ACC + another system. One respondent explicitly wants 'one CDE not two'.
market_demands.vitus_capability: Vitus layers on ACC. This is exactly the intelligence layer concept, but only for ACC.
market_demands.gap: Expanding to Dalux, Trimble Connect, and Projectwise would make Vitus the federated intelligence layer the market wants.

market_demands.name: AI-powered model checking and compliance
market_demands.description: Automated checking of BIM models against requirements, standards, and specifications using AI
market_demands.survey_evidence: Design compliance/model checking is the second most used AI application. Multiple 'dream AI' answers: 'automatic check of all models', 'compliance with standards', 'verify EIR satisfaction in real time'.
market_demands.vitus_capability: Display filter can check property presence, but no automated compliance or AI checking.
market_demands.gap: Opportunity for AI-powered EIR validation, automated property completeness checks, and standards compliance reports.

market_demands.name: AI-powered quantity takeoff
market_demands.description: Automated extraction and validation of quantities from BIM models and documents
market_demands.survey_evidence: Generative quantity takeoffs already used by some. 'Material quantity across all disciplines' and 'quantity surveying' are top automation wishes.
market_demands.vitus_capability: BIM property extraction, quantity takeoff via dashboards, calculated columns.
market_demands.gap: Not AI-enhanced. Could add cross-model aggregation, smart grouping, and PDF-to-quantity extraction.

market_demands.name: Carbon dashboards / LCA automation
market_demands.description: Environmental impact tracking, carbon footprint calculation, and lifecycle assessment from BIM data
market_demands.survey_evidence: 24% expect carbon dashboards to be standard by 2027. One respondent wants to automate carbon footprint as cross-project insight.
market_demands.vitus_capability: Dashboard engine exists. Calculated columns could compute carbon if data is present. But no built-in carbon factors or LCA logic.
market_demands.gap: Need material-to-carbon mapping, LCA calculation engine, and pre-built carbon dashboard templates.

market_demands.name: Digital twins with live telemetry
market_demands.description: Connected BIM models with real-time sensor/IoT data for operations and facility management
market_demands.survey_evidence: 24% expect digital twins to be standard by 2027. Multiple respondents on large infrastructure projects (500M+) see this as critical.
market_demands.vitus_capability: No IoT/sensor integration. Viewer could theoretically visualize live data via API.
market_demands.gap: Full gap. Would require IoT data ingestion, real-time visualization overlay, and operations dashboards.

market_demands.name: Design-to-construction data bridge
market_demands.description: Reducing friction and data loss in the handover from design teams to construction teams
market_demands.survey_evidence: 72% flag 'between design and construction teams' as a top friction point. The #1 data friction source across all respondents. Quotes: 'things in the model can be different to what is implemented on site', 'not all contractors work with the model'.
market_demands.vitus_capability: Shared views, collaboration tools, web-based access without installs. Custom data for progress tracking on objects.
market_demands.gap: Could strengthen with handover workflows, design-vs-as-built comparison tools, and contractor-specific simplified views.

market_demands.name: Data quality and consistency monitoring
market_demands.description: Real-time scoring and alerting on BIM data quality, completeness, and consistency across systems
market_demands.survey_evidence: Average data confidence only 7/10. 'Poor data quality/lineage' is a top AI barrier. Multiple respondents want 'data quality gap to EIR' as a dashboard metric. One wants 'informational completeness indicator against AIR'.
market_demands.vitus_capability: Display filter can identify missing properties. Dashboards can visualize completeness.
market_demands.gap: No automated quality scoring, no EIR-based validation rules, no quality trend tracking over time.

market_demands.name: Cross-project analytics and pattern recognition
market_demands.description: Aggregated intelligence across multiple projects to identify patterns, benchmarks, and recurring issues
market_demands.survey_evidence: 32% want to automate a cross-project insight. Examples: 'early recognition of patterns where errors often occur', 'detect systematic errors from one project to another', 'compare requirements across projects'.
market_demands.vitus_capability: Workspace with content browser. Can copy dashboards across projects. But no cross-project aggregation or analytics.
market_demands.gap: Need cross-project data aggregation, benchmarking, pattern detection, and portfolio-level dashboards.

market_demands.name: 4D scheduling integration
market_demands.description: Linking BIM models to project schedules for visual planning and progress tracking
market_demands.survey_evidence: 'Schedule variance' and 'planned vs reality' are top dashboard requests. Multiple respondents want 4D/5D linked to 3D. One specifically wants 'link schedules 4D and budget 5D with 3D geometry'.
market_demands.vitus_capability: Custom data could hold schedule dates on objects. Dashboards could visualize timeline. But no native schedule import or 4D playback.
market_demands.gap: Need schedule file import (P6/MS Project), model-to-task linking, timeline visualization, and schedule deviation tracking.
